steps.title: Determine when |x-5| = c has exactly one solution
steps.content: $$|x-5| = c$$ has: no solution if $c<0$, one solution if $c=0$ (giving $x=5$), two solutions if $c>0$. So exactly one solution requires $c=0$, i.e. $\frac{k}{2}=0$. Therefore $k/2$ must be $0$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
